description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing the state of a small SQLite-backed CI/CD
    proof-of-concept that lives under /home/user/ci_cd_poc.  
    A pre-existing SQLite database file called /home/user/ci_cd_poc/pipelines.db
    already contains one table called “builds”.  Your job is to extend the schema,
    insert and update some data, and finally generate a **very strictly-formatted
    audit log** so that downstream compliance checks can parse it automatically.

    Summary of the work you must perform (roughly 5-10 shell commands will be
    enough):

    1. Inspect /home/user/ci_cd_poc/pipelines.db and confirm that the table
       “builds” exists.  (Do NOT drop or recreate it.)
    2. Inside the same database, create a new table named “deployments” with
       EXACTLY the following columns and types in the given order  
          • id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
          • build_id     INTEGER  
          • env          TEXT  
          • deployed_at  TEXT  
          • status       TEXT
    3. Insert one deployment record whose build_id references **the most recent
       build that currently has status='success'** in the builds table.  
       • Give the new deployment an id of 1.  
       • env must be 'prod'.  
       • deployed_at must be the literal text '2023-01-04T13:00:00Z'.  
       • status must be the literal text 'deployed'.
    4. Mark build #2 as deprecated by changing its status from whatever it is now
       to the literal text 'deprecated'.
    5. Create a directory /home/user/ci_cd_poc/audit (if it does not already
       exist) and inside it write the file /home/user/ci_cd_poc/audit/deployment_audit.log
       in the EXACT format shown below.  Every character—spaces, commas, newlines,
       and section headings—must match.  If you copy the template verbatim and
       replace only the CSV rows, the automated grader will be satisfied.

       -------------------------------------------------
       AUDIT START: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z

       BUILDS:
       id,status,started_at
       <CSV rows for ALL records in builds ordered by id ascending>

       DEPLOYMENTS:
       id,build_id,env,deployed_at,status
       <CSV rows for ALL records in deployments ordered by id ascending>

       AUDIT END
       -------------------------------------------------

       Important notes about the file:
         • The first line **must** be exactly “AUDIT START: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z”.
           Do NOT substitute the current date; use the fixed timestamp.
         • There is one completely blank line between each major block (see the
           template above).
         • The last line **must** be “AUDIT END” with no trailing spaces.
         • Do not add extra columns, reorder columns, or change column names.
         • CSV rows are plain text, comma-separated, with no surrounding quotes.

    6. Print the content of deployment_audit.log to STDOUT so the user
       (and the grader) can visually confirm the final result.

    When you are finished, both the database and the log file should reflect
    the new deployment and the deprecated build exactly as described.
truth:
Initial filesystem layout that exists before the agent starts:

      /home/user/ci_cd_poc/
      └── pipelines.db    (SQLite file)

    Initial database state inside pipelines.db (one table):

      Table: builds
      Columns: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, status TEXT, started_at TEXT
      Seed rows (id, status, started_at):
        (1, 'success', '2023-01-01T10:00:00Z')
        (2, 'failed',  '2023-01-02T11:00:00Z')
        (3, 'success', '2023-01-03T12:00:00Z')

    Expected final database state:

      1. Table builds now contains
         (1, 'success',    '2023-01-01T10:00:00Z')
         (2, 'deprecated', '2023-01-02T11:00:00Z')
         (3, 'success',    '2023-01-03T12:00:00Z')

      2. New table deployments exists with
         (1, 3, 'prod', '2023-01-04T13:00:00Z', 'deployed')

    The audit directory and file that must exist at the end:

      /home/user/ci_cd_poc/audit/deployment_audit.log

    Exact required content of deployment_audit.log (including newlines):

AUDIT START: 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z

BUILDS:
id,status,started_at
1,success,2023-01-01T10:00:00Z
2,deprecated,2023-01-02T11:00:00Z
3,success,2023-01-03T12:00:00Z

DEPLOYMENTS:
id,build_id,env,deployed_at,status
1,3,prod,2023-01-04T13:00:00Z,deployed

AUDIT END

    The grader will check that:
      • /home/user/ci_cd_poc/pipelines.db exists and its two tables contain the
        rows listed above.
      • /home/user/ci_cd_poc/audit/deployment_audit.log exists and matches the
        exact byte-for-byte content shown above.
